summary: Classic vector-RAG baseline (fixed-size chunker + local nomic-embed-text embeddings via Ollama + cosine-similarity retrieval) independently reproduced and verified. Grid-search tuning claim confirmed genuine by re-running select_chunk_config myself against the same fixture corpus/queries -- results match self-consistency.json exactly, including the one discriminating candidate (220,33)=0.9. Ollama-only guarantee holds (hardcoded localhost:11434, no env-driven provider, no .env file, no openrouter/gemini references outside disclosure prose). No new pip dependency (pyproject.toml unchanged). Only the 3 claimed files plus CDR run artifacts were touched. Target test file: 12/12 passed independently (including live, non-mocked Ollama calls). Broader eval/ suite: all green. One substantive fairness-methodology concern raised (see dimension findings) regarding the tie-break rule's bias toward the leanest/zero-overlap config when candidates tie, which is honestly disclosed but not fully mitigated -- recommend a follow-up safeguard before the real-corpus benchmark subtask (5.3.4) consumes these defaults uncritically. Pre-existing, unrelated protobuf gencode/runtime version-mismatch failures found in ingestion/query gRPC test modules during full-repo regression re-run; confirmed unrelated to this diff (no changed file touches those modules; failures are present in local environment regardless of this commit and stem from environment drift since a prior protobuf-pin fix, not from this subtask's changes).

requirements_conformance.evidence: Acceptance criteria (real fixed-size-chunk baseline, actual chunk size/overlap tuning, embedding-based retrieval) all met. agents/eval/baselines/vector_rag.py implements chunk_document (word-boundary-snapped sliding window), OllamaEmbeddingClient (real nomic-embed-text via /api/embed), VectorRagIndex.search (cosine similarity), retrieve_documents (doc-level aggregation), select_chunk_config (grid search), recall_at_k (metric). Test spec satisfied: test_retrieve_documents_reasonable_recall_on_fixture_queries runs retrieval against fixture corpus+queries and asserts recall >= 0.75.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/ingestion-agent.md line 43-44 independently confirmed to already state nomic-embed-text as the intended local-embedding choice, matching the claim. Module correctly scoped to agents/eval/baselines/ only, does not modify agents/llm/LLMClient (documented rationale for a separate small client is reasonable given the differing call shape: embed(texts)->vectors vs complete(prompt)->str). Output shapes (doc_id strings, RawDocument (id,text) tuple shape) match ingestion.rawdoc.RawDocument and ground_truth.RelevantDoc per docstring claims (not independently cross-checked against those modules' actual field names, but plausible given consistent naming conventions elsewhere in the diff).

benchmark_fairness_special_scrutiny.evidence: Independently re-ran select_chunk_config(_FIXTURE_DOCS, _FIXTURE_QUERIES, OllamaEmbeddingClient(), top_k=3) myself: winning=(80,0), all 9 candidates identical to self-consistency.json's table (8 tied at 1.0 recall@3, only (220,33)=0.9). This proves the sweep is REAL and exercised (not decorative/hardcoded) -- confirmed by independent reproduction, not just reading the code. HOWEVER: because 8/9 candidates tie at perfect recall on this small 5-doc/5-query fixture, the tie-break rule (prefer smaller chunk_size, then smaller overlap) is doing essentially all the work of selecting (80,0) -- the sweep did not empirically demonstrate 0%-overlap's competitiveness, it merely failed to demonstrate its inferiority at this scale. The tie-break heuristic itself mechanically favors the leanest/least-resourced config whenever there is no discriminating signal, which is the same direction a corner-cutting/strawman implementation would land on by default, even though this implementation reached it via a legitimate, reproducible process. This is honestly disclosed in-file (module docstring, self-consistency.json's 'disclosed_limitation' field explicitly states this is not a claim that (80,0) is globally optimal and that a future real-corpus rerun is expected). Zero-overlap chunking is also a legitimate, common real-world baseline choice (not inherently a strawman) -- the issue's acceptance criteria requires 'actual tuning', which was genuinely performed, not that overlap must be nonzero. Judgment: this is an appropriately-disclosed scope-limited finding, not a defect, PROVIDED the future real-corpus benchmark subtask (5.3.4-style) is required to re-run select_chunk_config against the real corpus before drawing comparative conclusions -- this module does not itself enforce that re-run, which is a maintainability/process gap worth flagging.
benchmark_fairness_special_scrutiny.recommendation: Before subtask 5.3.4 (or whichever subtask wires this into the real Bitext/Enron/synthetic-PDF benchmark) consumes DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_WORDS/DEFAULT_OVERLAP_WORDS directly, require it to re-run select_chunk_config against the real corpus rather than importing the fixture-tuned constants uncritically. Consider also documenting/reconsidering the tie-break direction (prefer-leanest) since it systematically favors the weaker option among ties, which is the wrong default bias for a fairness-sensitive baseline.

regression_risk.evidence: git show --stat confirms only agents/eval/baselines/__init__.py (new, empty), agents/eval/baselines/vector_rag.py (new), agents/eval/test_vector_rag_baseline.py (new), plus 7 CDR artifact files under .cdr/runs/2026-07-11/10009-implementation/ were touched. No existing file modified. agents/pyproject.toml confirmed unchanged. Independently re-ran `cd agents && python -m pytest eval/ -q` -- all green. Independently re-ran full-repo `python -m pytest -q`: found 8 failures + 2 collection errors, but ALL in ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py, ingestion/test_shortlist.py, query/test_server.py, query/test_wiring.py, test_hivemind_pb2_smoke.py -- all due to a pre-existing 'google.protobuf.runtime_version.VersionError: gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6' environment mismatch, unrelated to any file touched by this diff. Confirmed via `git log -- agents/pyproject.toml` that a prior commit (b32042e) already had to pin protobuf for exactly this class of issue, indicating local environment drift rather than a regression introduced by 573478a. This discrepancy from the implementer's claimed '386/386 passed, zero regressions' could not be independently reproduced in the current environment, but is attributable to environment drift, not to this diff -- flagged for visibility, not held against the verdict.

edge_cases.evidence: chunk_document tested for: empty/whitespace-only text (returns []), exact-multiple-of-chunk-size (no dangling empty chunk), invalid overlap>=chunk_size (ValueError), chunk_size<=0 (ValueError), never-bisects-a-word invariant. recall_at_k tested for: perfect/partial match, k-cutoff truncation, vacuously-satisfied empty relevant set. VectorRagIndex.search handles empty chunk list. All independently re-run and passed.

security.evidence: grep for openrouter/gemini/api-key/.env across both new files returns matches only within disclosure prose (docstring text explicitly stating neither is used), not in any import/URL/credential. OllamaEmbeddingClient's base_url/model are hardcoded constants (DEFAULT_BASE_URL='http://localhost:11434', DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL='nomic-embed-text'), not environment-variable-driven, matching the elevated-scrutiny requirement from 5.1.2/5.1.3. Confirmed no .env file exists anywhere in the repo (find . -iname '.env*' returned nothing).

performance.evidence: _cosine_similarity is pure-Python (no numpy), and retrieve_documents defaults chunk_pool_size to len(index.chunks) (i.e., scores ALL chunks for every query) -- both explicitly and correctly disclosed in-code as fine at fixture scale but a known future-scale caveat (architecture-discovery.md). Acceptable for this subtask's fixture-only scope; would need revisiting (numpy/ANN index) before running against the real synthetic-PDF/Bitext/Enron corpora in a later subtask.

maintainability.evidence: Code is clearly structured, well-documented, dataclasses used appropriately, consistent naming with existing ingestion.rawdoc/ground_truth module conventions. Minor: retrieve_documents docstring's chunk_pool_size paragraph is slightly garbled/hard to parse ('Defaults to max(top_k*5, len(index.chunks))'s smaller bound is not applied here') -- functionally clear from the code but could be reworded for clarity.

test_coverage.evidence: 12/12 tests in agents/eval/test_vector_rag_baseline.py independently re-run and passed (6 pure-unit, no network; 4 live tests requiring real reachable local Ollama + nomic-embed-text, confirmed genuinely live not mocked by independently invoking OllamaEmbeddingClient().embed() directly and confirming distinct non-zero vectors from the live model). test_select_chunk_config_grid_search_matches_shipped_defaults independently re-run outside pytest via direct script invocation, confirming winning_config==(80,0) and the full recall table matches self-consistency.json exactly -- this is strong evidence the grid search is genuinely exercised, not decorative. Coverage gap: no test asserts behavior when Ollama returns embeddings in a different order/count mismatch scenario beyond the malformed-response-shape check in embed(); this is a minor coverage gap, not blocking given the module's disclosed fixture-only scope.

confidence.evidence: All claimed artifacts (chunker, embedding client, retrieval, grid search, fairness disclosure, Ollama-only guarantee, no new pip dependency, file-touch scope, target test pass count) were independently reproduced by re-running code and tests myself against the live local Ollama server, not merely read from the implementer's self-consistency.json. The one discrepancy found (full-repo regression run) was independently diagnosed as pre-existing environment drift unrelated to this diff's files.

non_blocking_findings: Tie-break rule in select_chunk_config (prefer smaller chunk_size then smaller overlap among ties) mechanically favors the leanest/zero-overlap config whenever the fixture corpus fails to discriminate (8/9 candidates tied at 1.0 here) -- recommend requiring a real-corpus re-run of select_chunk_config before any future benchmark subtask treats DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_WORDS=80/DEFAULT_OVERLAP_WORDS=0 as final, and consider documenting/revisiting the tie-break bias direction.

Full-repo regression suite currently fails in this environment due to a pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch in ingestion/query gRPC test modules, unrelated to this diff's files -- flagged for environment hygiene, not attributable to subtask 5.2.1.
retrieve_documents docstring's chunk_pool_size paragraph has slightly garbled phrasing; recommend a copy-edit pass.
